title: Technician guides patient through Optomap retinal imaging

background.chief_concern: Patient is here for routine retinal imaging and is willing to follow instructions

hidden_facts: The patient will generally follow instructions without offering much feedback
The patient will not usually recognize alignment or capture problems unless they are very obvious
The technician is expected to lead positioning, fixation, and any necessary retakes
learner_objective: Explain the Optomap retinal image briefly and clearly
Guide fixation and lid control as needed
Recognize poor captures and decide whether to retake them
Communicate retakes confidently without creating unnecessary concern
success_markers: Explains the test simply and confidently
Guides the patient into proper position before capture
Recognizes when the image quality is poor
Maintains patient trust during redirection or retakes
failure_patterns: Fails to notice poor image quality
Accepts a poor image and moves on
Does not manage eyelids appropriately when needed
Sounds uncertain when explaining a retake

learner_goal: Guide the patient into correct position
Recognize when the capture is poor
ideal_sequence: Explain the retinal image briefly in patient-friendly language
Direct the patient into correct position at the device
If image quality is poor, explain the need to retake and reposition the patient
Repeat until a usable image is obtained

common_patient_errors.description: Patient is too close to the device, leading to a distorted or unusable image
common_patient_errors.likely_trigger: learner gives incomplete or unclear positioning guidance
common_patient_errors.best_tech_response_goal: Recognize the poor capture and calmly reposition the patient for a retake

common_patient_errors.description: Patient is too far from the device, reducing image quality or capture accuracy
common_patient_errors.likely_trigger: learner does not guide the patient close enough before capture
common_patient_errors.best_tech_response_goal: Recognize the poor capture and guide the patient closer before retaking

common_patient_errors.description: Upper eyelid obstructs part of the retinal image
common_patient_errors.likely_trigger: learner does not manage lids or direct the patient appropriately
common_patient_errors.best_tech_response_goal: Recognize lid obstruction and give calm, clear instructions to improve the image

correction_rules.if_capture_is_poor: Patient remains neutral and assumes the test is complete unless guided otherwise
correction_rules.if_learner_recognizes_the_problem: Patient stays cooperative and follows repositioning instructions
correction_rules.if_learner_misses_the_problem: Patient does not question image quality and remains ready to move on
correction_rules.if_retake_is_needed: Patient accepts the redo if the technician communicates it calmly and clearly

instruction_quality_rules.condition: learner does not clearly guide patient position before capture and patient ends up too close
learner does not clearly guide patient position before capture and patient ends up too far away
learner does not address eyelid interference when needed

initial_view.description: Starting reference image for a usable Optomap capture

visual_events.internal_description: Captured image shows distortion from patient being too close
Captured image reflects patient being too far from the device
Captured image is obstructed by excessive eyelid
Captured image is affected by blinking
Patient positioning against the facepad is incorrect
